topic: Zero-day AI threat detection without code changes

pack.linkedin:
86% of companies deploying AI experienced a security incident in the past 12 months. Most of them were running detection built for a threat landscape that no longer exists.

The problem with signature-based and keyword-based defenses against AI-specific attacks is structural: they catch what you've already seen. Zero-day prompt injection variants, novel jailbreak constructions, and multi-vector attacks that combine context manipulation with encoding obfuscation don't appear in any prior dictionary. By the time a rule exists, the incident has already happened.

APRE's AI Threat Protection Shield takes a different approach. Semantic analysis works at the level of intent — understanding what a request is attempting, not just what tokens it contains. That distinction matters when you're facing 2,500+ daily AI threats, 67% of which have increased year-over-year in volume and sophistication.

When multiple threat categories trigger on the same request — say, a prompt injection attempt that also carries data exfiltration signatures — the composite risk score is amplified automatically: two categories add 10%, three add 20%, four or more add 40%. Coordinated campaigns that would defeat any single-category detector surface immediately.

Critically, none of this requires code changes. The 11-stage security pipeline sits inline between your application and your AI provider. Deployment takes under five minutes. Your engineering team keeps shipping; your security posture changes from day one.

Under EU AI Act Article 15, robustness against adversarial inputs is a compliance obligation, not an aspiration. Zero-day defense isn't a feature for high-risk environments only — it's the baseline.

How are you currently handling AI-specific threats your existing rules haven't seen yet?

#APIRE #AIActCompliance #CyberSecurity
pack.x: 67% YoY rise in AI-specific attacks. Signatures don't catch what they've never seen. Semantic intent analysis + multi-vector correlation does — deployed in <5 min, zero code changes. #AISecOps #NIS2
pack.hook: Your AI threat rules only catch yesterday's attacks.
